chapter_title.ar: بَابُ حَجِّ الْمُجَاوِرِينَ وَ قُطَّانِ مَكَّةَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 57 – The Hajj of the ones in the vicinity and residents of Makkah

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/206)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/206)
text: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ الْمُجَاوِرِ أَ لَهُ أَنْ يَتَمَتَّعَ بِالْعُمْرَةِ إِلَى الْحَجِّ قَالَ نَعَمْ يَخْرُجُ إِلَى مُهَلِّ أَرْضِهِ فَيُلَبِّي إِنْ شَاءَ .

en.hubeali: Al Husayn Bin Muhammad, from Moalla Bin Muhammad, from Al Hassan Bin Ali, from Aban Bin Usman, from Sama’at,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Al-Hassanasws, said, ‘I asked himasws about the one in the vicinity (of Makkah), is it for him that he should perform Tamatto with the Umra to the Hajj?’ Heasws said: ‘Yes. He should be going out to a place of its land, so he should be exclaiming Talbiyya if he so desires to’.
en.sarwar: 7. Al-Husayn ibn Muhammad has narrated from Mu‘alla’ ibn Muhammad from al-Hassan ibn Ali from Aban ibn ‘Uthman from Sama‘ah who has said the following: “I once asked abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who resides in Makkah: if he can perform al-‘Umrah of al-Hajj al-Tamattu‘ (advantageous). He (the Imam) said, ‘Yes, he can do so. He goes out to a Miqat (place for assuming the state of Ihram) to say Talbiyah, if he likes.’”
